meaning: n.(=keelman)驳船船员,用来测量液体的容器
examples: Keeler sipped at his gin and tonic.
Before Keeler it was Paulina, and before that I can't remember.
They flew Troy to Chicago and paid for a lengthy polygraph (lie detector) test at the Keeler polygraph Institute.
The video was graphic enough to prompt police to press charges against Keeler, the 55-year-old director of a local English school.
